home.title: PloyKit
home.subtitle: A single-version module host: the public site, auth, workspace, and admin stay with the host while business capabilities plug in through the current module contract.
cards: Declare pages, APIs, jobs, data, and permissions from module.ts.
Auth, workspace, admin, billing, and files are owned by the host.
Business modules implement their own pages, handlers, and product logic.

about.title: About PloyKit
about.subtitle: PloyKit is a single-version, contract-first host for modular products.
about.body: It keeps auth, workspace, admin, runtime storage, jobs, files, and commercial foundations on the host side, while business differences are expressed through the current module contract. The goal is not more abstraction; it is a boundary that both developers and AI can read from one module.ts.

highlights.title: Single-version mindset
highlights.body: No old contract compatibility story is shown to users; the current repository is the current fact surface.

highlights.title: Modules add only the increment
highlights.body: Modules declare pages, APIs, jobs, data, and permissions while shared capabilities stay with the host runtime.

highlights.title: Built for AI collaboration
highlights.body: Explicit contracts, capability docs, and diagnostic commands let models guess less and make verifiable changes.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.subtitle: PloyKit isolates data by product, workspace, and module boundaries by default.
privacy.body: Before production, replace this placeholder with real data collection, file storage, log, and audit retention policies.

terms.title: Terms
terms.subtitle: When using a PloyKit host, modules, data, and external providers should follow the service boundaries of the deployment owner.
terms.body: This page records the usage boundaries for accounts, credits, files, public tools, module capabilities, and service connections.

success.title: Success
success.subtitle: Your request has been completed.
success.body: Return to the dashboard to keep reviewing tasks, files, billing, or module runtime status.

docs.title: Docs
docs.subtitle: Read by path: understand the host, write a module, or validate delivery.
cards: Declare pages, APIs, jobs, data, permissions, and host capabilities from module.ts.
Understand which capabilities belong to the host and which code should stay inside a module.
Use modules:check, module:doctor, module:test, and browser checks to validate delivery quality.

start.title: Start from the current contract
start.body: Confirm host capabilities and module.ts fields before implementation, so boundaries are explicit before code is written.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Validate entitlements, credits, and orders from the current billing catalog; local development can use local checkout.

flow.title: Run the flow first, connect payments later
flow.body: Use local checkout during development to validate orders, entitlements, and credit flow. When you are ready to charge, connect the same catalog to Stripe.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Tell us about your module boundary, host capability needs, or deployment plan.
contact.received: Your message was received. We will continue by email.
contact.failed: Message submission failed. Check required fields and try again.
contact.name: Name
contact.messagePlaceholder: Briefly describe your module, host capability needs, deployment environment, or product plan.

aside.title: Tell us what you want to build
aside.body: Use this for module contracts, host capabilities, deployment planning, or commercialization questions. We record the request and handle it by email.

hero.subtitle: PloyKit keeps the public site, auth, workspace, admin, runtime storage, and commercial capabilities on the host side. Modules declare pages, APIs, jobs, data, and permissions through the current module.ts, so developers and AI share one boundary.
hero.stackIntro: The current implementation is built around Next.js, TypeScript, Postgres/Memory Store, and the module runtime, taking products from local validation to deployable releases.

trustItems.title: Single-version contract
trustItems.body: No old-contract compatibility story: development, diagnostics, and docs center on the current module.ts.

trustItems.title: Host capabilities
trustItems.body: Auth, workspace, files, billing, notifications, and workers are handled by the host runtime.

trustItems.title: Verifiable runtime
trustItems.body: Module scan, Doctor, tests, and runtime evidence stay on one validation path.

trustItems.title: Open and controllable
trustItems.body: Host, modules, docs, and product pages live in-repo for review and adaptation.

features.title: Why this works for AI-assisted development
features.body: AI does not need to understand the entire platform first; it can start from the current module contract and host capabilities.

cards.title: Contract-first modules
cards.body: Pages, APIs, data, jobs, permissions, and events live in one declaration, so AI does not have to guess the project shape.

cards.title: Smaller boundaries
cards.body: Module code stays in the module directory, host capabilities are called through ctx, and cross-layer edits are easier to spot.

cards.title: Clear validation path
cards.body: After generation, scan, Doctor, module tests, and page checks can trace issues back to concrete contract fields.

capabilities.title: The host carries the base; modules add business logic
capabilities.body: Auth, routing, data, files, jobs, events, and diagnostics are handled by the host. Modules declare what they need, then run, trace, and validate through one runtime.

cards.title: Data & schema
cards.body: Declare data models, migrations, and access boundaries while the host handles storage, scope, and runtime mapping.

cards.title: Pages & routes
cards.body: Modules declare public, workspace, and admin entries, then the host mounts and frames them consistently.

cards.title: Jobs & events
cards.body: Jobs, webhooks, business events, and result tracking flow through the host runtime.

cards.title: Files & artifacts
cards.body: Uploads, storage, permissions, and module-generated files are managed by the host.

cards.title: Service calls
cards.body: AI, email, payments, and external APIs are provided to modules as host capabilities.

cards.title: Module Doctor
cards.body: Scan contracts, permissions, routes, dependencies, and source boundaries to catch integration risks early.

composition.title: Start with one module, not a pile of adapters
composition.body: Declare what the module needs, then implement pages, handlers, and data. Only real business needs should enter the module.

steps.title: Declare
steps.body: Declare pages, APIs, schema, jobs, and permissions through the current contract.

steps.title: Call the host
steps.body: Use auth, workspace, file, billing, or event capabilities through ctx.

steps.title: Validate delivery
steps.body: Run scan, Doctor, tests, and browser checks until the module is real.

buildCards.title: Content module
buildCards.body: Content models, editor pages, publish APIs, and file capability.

buildCards.title: Resource module
buildCards.body: Data tables, list pages, detail pages, and permission boundaries.

buildCards.title: Public tool
buildCards.body: Public pages, lightweight APIs, and controlled anonymous access.

buildCards.title: Background work
buildCards.body: Jobs, events, webhooks, and run records.

buildCards.title: AI/RAG
buildCards.body: Text generation, retrieval, files, and usage metering.

buildCards.title: Operations admin
buildCards.body: Module state, runtime evidence, audit, and diagnostics.

final.title: Write the module contract first, then let the host run it.
final.body: Read the current docs, inspect a real module.ts, and plug your business capability into PloyKit. Common capabilities stay in the host while modules focus on product logic.

shellExperience.heroBody: PloyKit keeps module boundaries explicit while host capabilities handle shared product logic. Sign in to continue managing modules, workspaces, tasks, and deployments from the same product frame.

principles.title: Module contracts
principles.body: Pages, APIs, jobs, and data structures are declared around the active module.

principles.title: Host capabilities
principles.body: Auth, files, billing, events, and runtime work stay with the host base.

principles.title: One entry point
principles.body: Sign in, create an account, or reset access from one consistent entry point.

overview.title: Admin Overview
overview.subtitle: Focus on user growth, account risk, role coverage, and operating actions that need attention today.

risks.dataDetail: The host is using local memory mode. Move runtime state to Postgres before production traffic.
risks.deliveryDetail: {count} delivery records need review before the queue can be considered healthy.
risks.webhookDetail: {count} webhook receipts failed or were rejected. Inspect the receipt history.
risks.accessDetail: {count} users are suspended. Review their detail pages and audit timeline.
risks.readyDetail: Core signals are clear. Keep monitoring service health and recent activity.

today.title: Today
today.description: A compact pulse for daily operations.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure product identity, runtime settings, providers, theme boundaries, and diagnostics.

devConsole.title: Module Dev Console
devConsole.subtitle: Diagnostics-only workspace for module maps, bundle inspection, templates, AI prompts, and module:test reports.

modules.title: Modules
modules.subtitle: Operate installed product modules, lifecycle state, health, capabilities, and release readiness.

detail.subtitle: Module contract, routes, permissions, doctor, resource binding, gateways, and runtime activity details.
detail.missingBody: The current runtime did not find this module contract.
detail.productShapeDescription: Host-level product metadata shows which shells a module intends to own before raw route evidence is inspected.
detail.productShapeEmptyBody: This module can still be valid, but product modules should declare module.product so doctor, quality, and Admin can catch missing site, console, or admin surfaces.

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Manage accounts, access status, roles, and review queues for the product console.
users.reviewDescription: Only user states that need action appear here. Routine account records stay in the directory.
users.suspendedDescription: {count} users are suspended. Review account detail, audit trail, and restore only after confirming the reason.
users.pendingDescription: {count} users still need verification. Check whether invites, email delivery, or onboarding steps are blocked.
users.directoryDescription: Search accounts by email, user id, role, or workspace. Sensitive account changes live in the user detail page.
users.searchPlaceholder: Search email, user ID, role, or workspace

userDetail.title: User Detail
userDetail.subtitle: Review identity, roles, device sessions, password reset actions, and security audit timeline.
userDetail.actionsDescription: Use the smallest safe action. Role, status, session and password changes write audit records.
userDetail.accountStatusHint: Suspend only when access must stop immediately.
userDetail.hostRoleHint: Admin role grants host-level operations access.
userDetail.passwordResetHint: Generates a reset token and writes email provider and admin audit evidence.
userDetail.diagnosticsDescription: Sessions, audit, and raw metadata are folded by default; expand them for troubleshooting or evidence.
userDetail.activeSessionsDescription: Session revocation is a security action and should stay explicit.
userDetail.auditDescription: Recent security and account changes for this user.
userDetail.metadataDescription: Raw metadata is kept below the action surface for diagnostics.
userDetail.reviewRuleBody: Keep identity facts in the drawer; keep irreversible or risky changes in explicit action panels.
userDetail.missingBody: No matching host user was found.

rbac.title: Roles & Permissions
rbac.subtitle: Review system roles, host capabilities, module permissions, and access coverage. Admin module permissions can be 0 because host admins receive access through host capabilities.
rbac.roleManagementDescription: Review access by role first. System roles are review-only; custom roles can be created and maintained here once persistence is connected.
rbac.coverageEvidenceDescription: Use the matrix for coverage audits. It is folded by default so routine role work does not start as a permission-table scan.
rbac.panelDescription: Audit host capabilities, module permissions, and role coverage with inventories and the matrix.
rbac.hostInventoryDescription: {count} host capabilities grouped into category blocks.
rbac.moduleInventoryDescription: {count} module permissions grouped below host capabilities.

serviceConnections.title: Service Connections
serviceConnections.subtitle: Operate service providers, secret sources, health checks, and connector call logs.

runs.title: Runs
runs.subtitle: Monitor background execution, stalled work, failed attempts, and recovery actions.

runDetail.title: Run Detail
runDetail.subtitle: Run input, output, logs, events, usage, audit, correlation id, and recovery actions.
runDetail.missingBody: The current runtime store did not find this run.

webhooks.title: Webhooks / Outbox
webhooks.subtitle: Review delivery queue health, webhook receipts, worker lag, and replay actions.

webhookDetail.title: Outbox Detail
webhookDetail.subtitle: Outbox payload, attempts, errors, linked webhook receipts, and replay/discard actions.
webhookDetail.missingBody: The current runtime store did not find this outbox record.

billing.title: Billing
billing.subtitle: Operate plans, SKUs, orders, entitlements, credits, and payment evidence.
billing.creditReservationsDescription: Pre-authorized credit holds are visible beside the ledger so AI task reserve, commit, and release states can be audited.
billing.redeemCodeLifecycleDescription: Only masked code metadata and hash prefixes are exposed; raw redeem codes remain one-time export material.
billing.machineApiKeysDescription: Server-to-server access keys are listed by prefix and owner; hashes and raw keys are never exposed.
billing.riskFactsDescription: Risk events and subject blocks stay in the same commercial evidence view as the facts they protect.

revenue.title: Revenue
revenue.subtitle: Review paid orders, currency totals, provider events, and billing reconciliation.

entitlements.title: Entitlements
entitlements.subtitle: Review access grants, package source, manual overrides, and revocation actions.

usage.title: Usage
usage.subtitle: Module usage and metering ledger are summarized by meter, module, status, and product scope.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Operational metrics are summarized from runtime store, identity, files, and commercial ledger.

files.title: Files
files.subtitle: Govern uploaded files, storage durability, scan drift, quarantine, and retention actions.

fileDetail.title: File Detail
fileDetail.subtitle: File source, object information, download access, and governance audit.
fileDetail.missingBody: The current runtime store did not find this file.

audit.title: Audit
audit.subtitle: Review security-sensitive actions, actor scope, usage traces, and export evidence.

search.title: Search
search.subtitle: Global operations search across users, runs, files, orders, modules, and outbox.

error.title: Admin page failed to load
error.description: This page hit an unhandled exception. Retry the current operation first; if it keeps happening, inspect server logs and the latest module run.

inline.open_diagnostics_and_fix_doctor_errors_2: Open diagnostics and fix doctor errors.

inline.keep_traffic_paused_until_maintenance_ev: Keep traffic paused until maintenance evidence is clear.
inline.enable_when_this_product_capability_is_r: Enable when this product capability is ready.
inline.human_readable_phrases_derived_from_rout: Human-readable phrases derived from routes, surfaces, jobs, data, webhooks, actions, and permissions.
inline.the_next_action_is_derived_from_install_: The next action is derived from install state, lifecycle, runtime state, and diagnostics.
inline.use_this_before_enabling_traffic_or_prep: Use this before enabling traffic or preparing RC evidence.
inline.related_links_use_the_connection_id_modu: Related links use the connection id, module id, or service name as correlation evidence; jobs and events currently resolve through run kind filters and webhook/outbox evidence.
inline.inspect_runs_that_share_the_module_or_se: Inspect runs that share the module or service correlation key.
inline.jobs_do_not_have_a_standalone_admin_rout: Jobs do not have a standalone Admin route yet; the link resolves to the job run kind filter.
inline.inspect_outbox_receipt_and_dead_letter_r: Inspect outbox, receipt, and dead-letter records for the same module or service key.
inline.search_policy_test_rotation_and_retentio: Search policy, test, rotation, and retention audit by connection id.
inline.choose_the_connection_that_will_read_a_n: Choose the connection that will read a new secret reference.
inline.enter_env_name_after_the_secret_is_provi: Enter env:NAME after the secret is provisioned as an environment variable.
inline.run_test_from_the_provider_matrix_and_ve: Run Test from the provider matrix and verify audit evidence.
inline.rotate_the_secret_source_for_value_the_p: Rotate the secret source for {value1}? The plaintext secret will not be shown.
inline.clear_the_filter_return_to_modules_or_in: Clear the filter, return to modules, or inspect webhooks for queued and dead-letter records.
inline.trigger_worker_drain_now_this_run_proces: Trigger worker drain now? This run processes up to 25 current Jobs, Events, Webhooks, and Email outbox records.
inline.this_drain_action_is_the_host_worker_dra: This drain action is the host worker drain, not webhook-only; it processes Jobs, Events, Webhooks, and Email through the shared outbox.
inline.outbox_detail_keeps_runs_jobs_events_ser: Outbox detail keeps Runs, Jobs, Events, Service, and Audit links ahead of raw payload evidence.
inline.operations_commerce_and_platform_entry_p: Operations, commerce, and platform entry points are separated so the homepage sends people to the right place first.
inline.file_storage_is_using_value_configure_du: File storage is using {value1}. Configure durable object storage before user uploads matter.
inline.value_diagnostics_are_open_across_route_: {value1} diagnostics are open across route catalog, providers, retention, or security readiness.
inline.value_is_loaded_as_the_product_theme_pro: {value1} is loaded as the product theme profile.
inline.value_accepted_light_tokens_are_availabl: {value1} accepted light tokens are available to shell components.
inline.value_dark_tokens_are_available_for_dark: {value1} dark tokens are available for dark/system mode.
inline.value_rejected_tokens_or_diagnostics_nee: {value1} rejected tokens or diagnostics need review before release.
inline.visual_baseline_records_value_admin_them: Visual baseline records {value1} Admin theme screenshots and {value2} browser screenshots.
inline.block_release_and_repair_error_diagnosti: Block release and repair error diagnostics from the AI repair pack first.
inline.module_owner_reviews_warnings_and_test_e: Module owner reviews warnings and test evidence before release.
inline.standard_owner_review_keep_module_doctor: Standard owner review; keep module:doctor and module:test evidence current.
inline.mdc_does_not_have_a_remote_environment_c: MDC does not have a remote-environment contract yet; this compares runtime, module map, module:test evidence, and release target separately.
inline.module_owner_readme_runbook_escalation_a: Module owner, README runbook, escalation, and linked runs are visible before raw tables.
inline.each_module_has_a_copyable_repair_pack_w: Each module has a copyable repair pack with diagnostics, AI prompt, owner, runbook, and minimal validation commands.
inline.search_objects_jump_to_domains_and_keep_: Search objects, jump to domains, and keep recent lookups.
inline.search_users_modules_files_orders_runs_1: Search users, modules, files, orders, runs...
inline.search_recent_items_jump_to_admin_domain: Search recent items, jump to admin domains, or run a focused global lookup.
inline.no_value_match_this_query_press_enter_to: No {value1} match this query. Press Enter to open the full search page.

default.title: PloyKit
default.description: PloyKit is a single-version module host with a public site, auth, workspace, admin, and module runtime.

home.title: PloyKit current module host
home.description: Declare pages, APIs, jobs, data, and permissions through the current module contract, then run and validate them through the host.

about.title: About PloyKit
about.description: Learn PloyKit's single-version contract, host boundary, and module development model.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.description: Validate the current billing catalog, entitlements, credits, and local checkout flow.

docs.title: Docs
docs.description: PloyKit documentation for the current module contract, host boundary, and runtime validation.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Contact the product team about module systems, host operations, and deployment plans.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Understand the data and privacy principles of the PloyKit product host.

terms.title: Terms
terms.description: Review the terms for using the PloyKit product host.

success.title: Success
success.description: The request has completed; return to the workspace to continue with tasks, files, billing, or module status.
